hero.name: Tu N (Alex) Tran
hero.headline: Graduate student in Engineering Data Science building intelligent tools for biological and multimodual data analysis.

featured.title: Loki – AI-Powered Nuclei Segmentation Platform
bullets: Engineered frontend UI for AI inference on H&E images.
Enabled one-click model execution without requiring Python.
Built interactive region selection directly on tissue images.
Developed AI chatbox for real-time biological insights, identifying cell types and tissue structures.
Significantly improved accessibility for non-technical users in research settings.

paragraphs: I am a Graduate student in Engineering Data Science with experience applying statistical modeling, machine learning, and data visualization to biological and engineering problems.

I am highly skilled in Python, R, SQL (in progress), and cloud-based workflows. My core expertise lies in building end-to-end pipelines, cleaning and structuring large datasets, and developing precise interactive dashboards.
Currently, I am seeking a Data Science internship to leverage my analytical skills and programming expertise for data-driven decision making.

education.degree: Master of Science in Engineering Data Science
education.school: University of Houston, Cullen College of Engineering

projects.title: Mjolnir
projects.description: Designed and deployed a visualization website using Python (Dash, Plotly) for interactive exploration of multimodal datasets. Integrated gigapixel histology images with gene expression and pathway enrichment results. Co-authored a Research Square preprint.

projects.title: Visual–Omics Curation
projects.description: Curated a multimodal dataset linking histology images with spatial gene expression across 32 organs. Built diagnostics to evaluate machine learning models, heavily contributing to a Nature Methods publication on foundation models.

projects.title: Macrophage Analysis
projects.description: Applied Leiden clustering and UMAP to single-cell data for cell-type identification. Conducted marker gene analysis to assess treatments. Automated preprocessing pipelines in Python.

projects.title: COVID-19 Analysis
projects.description: Analyzed RNA-seq data with TopHat, Cuffdiff, and Python. Conducted differential expression analysis and visualized results with heatmaps and volcano plots. Built a custom Gene Ontology tool.

experience.company: Houston Methodist, Dr. Guangyu Wang Lab
bullets: Built reproducible pipelines for large-scale single-cell and spatial data analysis using Python, R, and Snakemake.
Applied clustering, trajectory inference, and statistical testing to generate insights from complex datasets.
Created interactive dashboards (Dash/Plotly) to enable exploratory analysis by cross-functional teams.
Delivered analyses supporting a Nature Methods publication and maintained the lab’s public-facing research website.
Monitored digital engagement metrics with Google Analytics and optimized campaigns for +39.5% performance improvement.
Designed and launched targeted LinkedIn, Facebook, and Google Ads campaigns, raising user engagement by 50%.
Produced clear, data-driven reports to inform leadership and refine product strategy.

publications.title: A visual–omics foundation model to bridge histopathology with spatial transcriptomics
publications.authors: Chen W., Zhang P., Tran T. N., et al. (2025). Nature Methods.

publications.title: Thor: a platform for cell-level investigation of spatial transcriptomics and histology
publications.authors: Zhang P., Chen W., Tran T. N., et al. (2025). Nature Communications, 16, 7178.

items: Python (Pandas, NumPy, SciPy, scikit-learn, Plotly, Dash)

contact.paragraph: I am seeking a Data Science internship to leverage my analytical skills and programming expertise for data-driven decision making. Let's connect!
